name: JAX (Google DeepMind)

auth.notes: Local library; Google Cloud TPU access requires GCP credentials

performance.notes: XLA compilation; TPU-native; JIT compilation after first call; very fast on hardware

agent_readiness.notes: Google's NumPy-compatible ML framework with composable function transformations. jit() for JIT compilation, vmap() for vectorization, grad() for automatic differentiation, pmap() for multi-device parallelism. XLA compilation. TPU support (unique among ML frameworks). Functional programming paradigm — no mutable state. Used by DeepMind, Google Brain, and top ML researchers. Flax and Equinox build neural networks on top of JAX.

gotchas: Functional paradigm requires mindset shift (no in-place mutation)
PyTorch ecosystem much larger (more pretrained models)
GPU setup more complex than PyTorch
avoid_when: Deploying pretrained transformer models (PyTorch/HuggingFace ecosystem better)
Teams not familiar with functional ML programming
summary: JAX is Google's NumPy-compatible ML framework with composable function transforms: jit() for compilation, vmap() for vectorization, grad() for autodiff, pmap() for device parallelism. XLA backend enables TPU and GPU acceleration. Functional paradigm. Used heavily in research (DeepMind, Google Brain). Best for TPU training, custom hardware-efficient operations, and research requiring composable transformations.
